front: Play: set on a subway car; a white woman enters eating an apple and calls a black man 'Uncle Thomas Woolly-Head' before stabbing him
Play: a woman dances mock-seductively saying 'do the nasty. Rub bellies' on a subway car
Play: after the protagonist is stabbed, the woman orders passengers to throw out the body and contemplates her next victim
Poem: asks 'Who told 4000 Israeli workers at the Twin Towers / To stay home that day'; led to abolition of the NJ Poet Laureate position
Poem: speaker counts the stars every night and sees his daughter praying in her room; begins 'Lately, I've become accustomed to the way the ground opens up'
back: "Preface to a Twenty Volume Suicide Note" (Amiri Baraka)
work: Preface to a Twenty Volume Suicide Note

front: Work: study of 'Negro Music in White America' that maps the shift from rural blues to urban jazz; includes chapter 'Swing-From Verb to Noun'
Poem: 'Poems are bullshit unless they are teeth or trees or lemons piled on a step'; declares 'we want poems that kill'
Author: founded the Black Arts Repertory Theater, attracting artists like Sonia Sanchez and Sun Ra; changed name from LeRoi Jones in 1967
Play: Walker Vessels is injured by an explosion implied to have killed his two 'freakish mulatto' daughters with ex-wife Grace
